title: Compliance Scan
findings: Found 3 concrete compliance issues: raw session digest retention, missing session/actor provenance on review lifecycle events, and an archive secret-scan gap for persisted path metadata.

issues_found.title: Session digests persist raw user transcript snippets without redaction or retention controls
issues_found.description: Stop-hook transcript summarization pushes raw user message text into user_messages, then session-digest-writer writes up to 10 messages of 500 chars each under .fabric/.cache/session-digests. The writer only truncates; it does not call the existing secret/PII redaction utilities and no TTL cleanup is configured for these digest files, so sensitive prompts can linger in local cache and later feed fabric-archive context.
issues_found.fix_direction: Run digest content through the shared redactSecrets/privacy filter before write, add a configurable TTL/cleanup for .fabric/.cache/session-digests, and prefer summaries over verbatim user-message bullets.

issues_found.title: fab_review lifecycle events lack invoker session provenance
issues_found.description: FabReviewInputSchema branches for approve/reject/defer contain only pending paths/reason/until and no session_id or actor field; the service then emits knowledge_proposed, knowledge_promote_started, knowledge_promoted, knowledge_rejected, and knowledge_deferred events with timestamp/reason but no session_id/correlation_id. The ledger records that a knowledge write happened but cannot attribute it to the invoking session or operator.
issues_found.fix_direction: Add optional session_id/correlation_id (and, if available, actor/client) to fab_review input, thread it through reviewKnowledge, and include it on all review mutation events; add tests asserting approve/reject/defer/promote events preserve provenance.

issues_found.title: Archive secret scan omits persisted path metadata fields
issues_found.description: extractKnowledge gates secret-shaped content by scanning user_messages_summary, session_context, must_read_if, and intent_clues only. However recent_paths are written verbatim into the pending entry Evidence block and evidence_paths are written into frontmatter, so credential-like or PII-bearing path strings can still land in a git-backed knowledge store despite the comment that secrets must never be persisted.
issues_found.fix_direction: Include recent_paths, evidence_paths, relevance_paths, tags, tech_stack, and impact in the secret/privacy scan before rendering; redact or reject sensitive path metadata and add regression tests covering token-looking paths.
